title: Piptanthocereus lamprospermus
intro: Piptanthocereus lamprospermus, also known as the golden candle cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family (Cactaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Piptanthocereus lamprospermus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Piptanthocereus lamprospermus is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.

This genus is a part of the larger family of cacti, which includes over 1,500 species of plants.
